summary: DHH reviews the Minisforum UM870, a $550 mini PC that he finds surprisingly capable as a developer workstation. Running Linux, the tiny AMD-powered machine completes HEY's full test suite faster than a $2,199 M4 Pro Mac Mini, thanks largely to native Docker support. He argues that AMD's chips, manufactured on the same TSMC 5nm process as Apple's M2, deliver comparable single-core performance at a fraction of the cost. The post celebrates how small, cheap, and powerful developer machines have become, positioning Linux and AMD as compelling alternatives to Apple's ecosystem.
key_points: A $550 Minisforum UM870 with AMD 8745H beats a $2,199 M4 Pro Mac Mini on HEY's test suite due to native Docker/Linux advantages
AMD's Zen 4 cores, built on the same TSMC 5nm process as Apple's M2, match Apple's single-core Geekbench performance at a quarter the price
Mac's Docker performance suffers from filesystem virtualization overhead, giving Linux a significant real-world advantage for containerized development workflows
The upcoming AMD Ryzen AI Max 395+ in the Framework Desktop completes HEY tests more than twice as fast as the M4 Pro
Synthetic benchmarks like Geekbench can be misleading — real-world workload performance depends heavily on the specific software stack and OS
money_quotes: I'm shocked to report that I really don't need more than this!
Somehow I thought Apple had some special sauce that made this possible, and that PCs were forever condemned to be bigger, louder, and slower. How 2020 of me.
The UM870 is a little beast. It runs our full HEY test suite in just 2m28s. In comparison, it takes a 14-core M4 Pro 2m49s, and such an Apple costs $2,199.
At less than a quarter the cost, it's hard to complain.
I love that open-source Linux is able to run laps around Apple in the workloads that many developers need.
I love that this tiny little silent $550 mini PC on my desk is capable of putting out computing power that only a decade ago would have been reserved to loud, honking metal in a data center.
The irony is that it was partly Apple's volume with and investment in TSMC that got us these incredible AMD chips.
key_insight: Cheap AMD mini PCs running Linux now outperform Apple hardware costing four times as much for real-world developer workloads, thanks to native Docker support and TSMC manufacturing advances.
